title: pittosporum ellipticum
intro: Pittosporum ellipticum, also known as the Tī Kōuka, is a native New Zealand tree species that belongs to the Pittosporaceae family. Its bark and leaves have been used in traditional Maori medicine for various purposes, including treating colds and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and providing antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Pittosporum ellipticum are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and fever.
